name: Modisa Maphanyane
bio: Modisa founded MobiRides with a vision to revolutionize transportation in Botswana. With over 18 years of experience in the automotive and tech industries, he leads our mission to make car sharing accessible and sustainable for all Batswana.

name: Awumsuri Mtui
bio: Awumsuri brings cutting-edge technology solutions to MobiRides. He specializes in mobile app development, IoT integration, and building scalable platforms that serve thousands of users across Botswana.

name: Ella Mogotsi
bio: Ella ensures smooth daily operations across all MobiRides locations. With a background in logistics and fleet management, she optimizes our vehicle network and enhances user experience throughout Botswana.

name: Arnold Bathoen
bio: Arnold is a seasoned engineer who leads development of MobiRides' mobile applications and backend systems. With deep expertise in scalable architecture and full-stack development, he drives technical excellence and mentors junior engineers across the team.

name: Kelvin Mhlambi
bio: Kelvin leads MobiRides' creative vision, shaping the brand identity and visual storytelling across all platforms. His bold design thinking and deep understanding of the African market ensure every touchpoint reflects the energy and ambition of MobiRides.

name: Refilwe Nttshambiwa
bio: Refilwe leads growth at MobiRides, focused on expanding reach and strengthening user engagement. She believes in building bridges between human-centered service and modern tools. Whether coordinating schedules, supporting leadership, or learning how data can empower decision-making, she aims to be a quiet force behind meaningful progress.

name: Loago Kaelo
bio: Loago provides oversight for the MarOps and Development teams, leading growth, partnerships, technical coordination, and strategic projects. He supports leadership decisions across fundraising, shareholder and advisor relations, hiring, and go-to-market execution. His recent initiatives include managing MobiRides' partnership with Pay-U Technologies to support insurance integration, developing telematics and vehicle-tracking partnerships to strengthen risk management, increasing inbound interest by 1,900%, growing platform sign-ups by 29%, scaling social media presence by 140%, and supporting fundraising readiness through investor communications, pitch deck refinement, and strategic stakeholder engagement.

name: Tapologo Nicholas Moilwa
bio: Tapologo is a passionate software engineering intern contributing to MobiRides' platform development. Currently studying Computer Science, he brings fresh ideas and a strong foundation in modern web technologies, actively supporting the engineering team in building features that serve communities across Botswana.
